mcqs.question: A fair six-sided die is rolled once. What is the probability distribution of the outcome?
mcqs.explanation: The outcome can only take on a finite number of distinct values (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6), and each value has an equal probability of 1/6. This defines a discrete uniform distribution.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a key property of *any* probability mass function (PMF) P(X=x) for a discrete random variable X?

options.text: The sum of all P(X=x) must be 1
The PMF must be a continuous function
P(X=x) must be the same for all values of x

mcqs.explanation: The fundamental property of a PMF is that the sum of the probabilities for all possible outcomes in the sample space must equal 1. This ensures the total probability is accounted for.

mcqs.question: If P(A) = 0.4 and P(B) = 0.3, and A and B are mutually exclusive events, what is P(A ∪ B)?
mcqs.explanation: For mutually exclusive events, P(A ∪ B) = P(A) + P(B) = 0.4 + 0.3 = 0.7. We can simply add the probabilities because the events cannot occur simultaneously.

mcqs.question: Two independent events A and B have probabilities P(A) = 0.5 and P(B) = 0.6. What is P(A ∩ B)?
mcqs.explanation: For independent events, P(A ∩ B) = P(A) × P(B) = 0.5 × 0.6 = 0.30. Independence means the occurrence of one event does not affect the other.

mcqs.question: Which of the following best describes a continuous random variable?

options.text: It can take any value within a specified range
It has a countable number of possible values
It always follows a normal distribution
Its probability is defined by a PMF rather than a PDF

mcqs.explanation: A continuous random variable can take any value within an interval or collection of intervals, unlike a discrete variable which has countable distinct values.
